title: Betencourtia stereophylla

intro: Betencourtia stereophylla, also known as Yerba de Monte or "mountain herb," is a shrub native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, belonging to the family Verbenaceae. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues and other conditions. The bioactive constituents of Betencourtia stereophylla include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Betencourtia stereophylla is often used to prepare teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as dried extracts and capsules, which are commonly used to support respiratory health and immune function.
